title: Sprint 3 — Contract Items & Economy
goals: Players have limited match contracts that deplete over time
Contract items can be applied to restore match eligibility
Contracts have 5 tiers (Bronze → Legend) with increasing value
Contract packs can be earned, bought, and opened like card packs
Match wins in all modes reward contract packs
Zero-contract players are blocked from playing XI

introduction.concept: Every player card in Cricket Ultimate Manager now has a match contract — a fixed number of matches they can play before needing a contract renewal. Contract items are consumables that add matches back. They come in 5 tiers, can be earned from match rewards, opened from contract packs, or bought on the market. This adds a light economy layer (like FIFA UT contracts) that drives engagement through pack opening, match replay, and inventory management.
designDecisions: Contracts are per UserCard instance (not per PlayerCard master data)
Default contract count varies by card rarity — higher rarity = more default matches
5 tiers: Bronze (+3), Silver (+7), Gold (+15), Elite (+30), Legend (+50)
Contracts are consumed on match completion, not at lineup selection (to avoid punishing the player if the match crashes)
Contract packs are a separate table from card packs (cleaner schema, independent probability tables)
Stacking: same-tier contracts stack in inventory (quantity column) to reduce DB rows
Card packs can also contain contract items as side drops alongside player cards

stories.title: Database schema for contracts system
stories.userStory: As a developer, I want the database schema to support contracts so that the backend can store and manage contract items properly.
acceptanceCriteria: New table `contract_types` with columns: id (UUID PK), name (TEXT), tier (TEXT), matches_awarded (INT), image_url (TEXT?), is_available (BOOLEAN), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ)

New table `user_contracts` with columns: id (UUID PK), user_id (UUID FK → users), contract_type_id (UUID FK → contract_types), quantity (INT DEFAULT 1), source (TEXT — reward/purchase/tournament/market/pack), acquired_at (TIMESTAMPTZ)

New table `user_contract_packs` with columns: id (UUID PK), user_id (UUID FK → users), pack_name (TEXT), contract_count (INT DEFAULT 3), bronze_chance (DOUBLE), silver_chance (DOUBLE), gold_chance (DOUBLE), elite_chance (DOUBLE), legend_chance (DOUBLE), source (TEXT — reward/purchase/tournament/level_up), opened (BOOLEAN DEFAULT false), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ)
ALTER TABLE user_cards ADD COLUMN contracts_remaining INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 7
ALTER TABLE user_cards ADD COLUMN contracts_max INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 7
INSERT seed data into `contract_types`: Bronze (+3), Silver (+7), Gold (+15), Elite (+30), Legend (+50)
INSERT seed data into `user_contract_packs`: Bronze Contract Pack (3-5 contracts, weighted to bronze/silver), Silver Contract Pack, Gold Contract Pack, Elite Contract Pack, Legend Contract Pack
Add database index on user_contracts(user_id) user_contracts(user_id, contract_type_id) user_contract_packs(user_id, opened) user_cards(user_id, contracts_remaining)
Enable RLS on all new tables with policies: users can only read/write their own rows
Follow existing migration naming: YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_contracts.sql
stories.technicalNotes: Contract types are seed data (like pack_types). user_contracts uses a quantity column for stacking duplicate contracts (same tier from same source). user_contract_packs mirrors user_card_packs but for contract-specific packs.
edgeCases: migration on existing user_cards — set contracts_remaining = 7 for all existing rows, contracts_max = 7
Quantity = 0 rows in user_contracts should be cleaned up or excluded from queries
User receives contract pack but has never played — contracts system should be invisible until the user has cards in their XI

stories.title: Dart models for contract entities
stories.userStory: As a developer, I want Dart model classes for ContractType, UserContract, and UserContractPack so that the app can parse and display contract data.
acceptanceCriteria: New `contract_model.dart` in lib/models/ with ContractType, UserContract, UserContractPack classes
ContractType: id (String), name (String), tier (String), matchesAwarded (int), imageUrl (String?), isAvailable (bool), fromJson(), toJson()
UserContract: id (String), userId (String), contractTypeId (String), quantity (int, default 1), source (String), acquiredAt (DateTime), contractType (ContractType?, joined), fromJson()

UserContractPack: id (String), userId (String), packName (String), contractCount (int, default 3), bronzeChance/silverChance/goldChance/eliteChance/legendChance (double), source (String), opened (bool, default false), createdAt (DateTime), fromJson()
Models exported from lib/models/models.dart
All models have const constructors, copyWith (where useful), and proper null safety
stories.technicalNotes: Follow UserCard/PackType pattern exactly. UserContract.contractType joins contract_types table. UserContractPack mirrors UserCardPack but for contract-only packs.
edgeCases: quantity should never be 0 or negative — validate in fromJson
tier is a simple string enum: 'bronze'/'silver'/'gold'/'elite'/'legend'

stories.title: Contract inventory provider — load, apply, and manage contracts
stories.userStory: As a player, I want to see my contract items and apply them to my players so that I can keep my squad match-ready.
acceptanceCriteria: New `contracts_provider.dart` in lib/providers/ with ContractsNotifier (StateNotifierProvider<ContractsNotifier, AsyncValue<List<UserContract>>>)
loadContracts() fetches from user_contracts table joined with contract_types, filtered by quantity > 0

openContractPack(packId) generates random contracts based on pack probabilities, inserts into user_contracts (or increments quantity of existing same-tier contract), marks pack as opened, returns list of awarded ContractType IDs

applyContract(userCardId, contractTypeId) decrements quantity of the contract, increments userCard.contracts_remaining by contractType.matchesAwarded, caps at contracts_max or contracts_max + matchesAwarded (extendable max)
getContractCount(contractTypeId) returns total quantity across all rows for that type
getApplicableContracts(userCard) returns list of valid contract types for this card
Provider respects SupabaseService pattern — uses client.rpc for atomic applyContract operation
Real-time refresh on contract changes via subscribeToContracts channel
Export from lib/providers/providers.dart
stories.technicalNotes: applyContract should be a server-side RPC for atomicity: decrement user_contracts.quantity, update user_cards.contracts_remaining, update user_cards.contracts_max if applicable. Opening a contract pack can be client-side (like card packs) — generate rarities, pick random contract_types, upsert into user_contracts. Contracts never expire — they stack infinitely.
edgeCases: Apply contract to a card with full contracts — show warning and prevent application
Apply higher-tier contract — the contracts_max should be updated if the new max exceeds old max (e.g., Gold on a card with contracts_max=15 increases it to 15+15=30)
If quantity reaches 0 after consumption, row should be deleted from user_contracts
If user has no contracts of any tier, show 'No contracts available' empty state
Apply contract should update local state immediately (optimistic) and reconcile with DB

stories.title: Contract consumption on match completion + XI validation
stories.userStory: As a player, I want every match to consume one contract from each XI player and be blocked from fielding expired players so that contracts matter.
acceptanceCriteria: On match completion (in _onMatchComplete or match_completion_handler), decrement contracts_remaining by 1 for each player in the playing XI
Contract consumption happens AFTER match simulation but BEFORE reward persistence
If a player's contracts_remaining reaches 0, they are marked as 'out of contracts'
Squad builder / match preview screen shows contract status for each XI player
Players with 0 contracts_remaining cannot be placed in the starting XI (validation on lineup submit)
If XI contains zero-contract players, show a warning dialog with the option to apply contracts or replace them
Contracts are NOT consumed for AI opponent players — only the user's XI
Contract consumption is stored via server-side RPC for atomicity (prevent double-spending on retry)
UI badges on player cards in squad builder: green (>=5), yellow (1-4), red (0 — expired) indicator
stories.technicalNotes: Hook into the existing match completion flow in match_completion_handler.dart. The lineup data is available via line_up table. For the RPC, pass the array of user_card_ids and decrement contracts_remaining for each. The existing `award_match_rewards` RPC is the model — but this should be a separate RPC for clarity. Squad validation happens in squad_builder_screen.dart's submit handler. The match_preview_screen.dart should check contracts before starting the match.
edgeCases: Match retry after failure — contract should not be consumed twice. Use the existing idempotency pattern.
Multiplayer matches — only the user's XI contracts are consumed, not the opponent's
Tournament matches — same as regular matches. Contracts consumed per round.
Challenge mode — contracts consumed normally
Match simulation (skip) — contracts still consumed (player was in XI)
Match abort / forfeit — should contracts be consumed? Decision: YES (player was selected, match started)
What if all XI players are out of contracts? Prevent match start until at least 11 valid players are fielded

stories.title: Contracts UI screens — inventory, apply, and player card integration
stories.userStory: As a player, I want a contracts inventory screen and the ability to apply contracts to my players so that I can manage my squad's match eligibility.
acceptanceCriteria: New ContractsScreen at /contracts with tabs: 'My Contracts' and 'Contract Packs'
'My Contracts' tab: grid of contract types with quantity badges, sorted by tier (legend first)
Each contract card shows: tier badge, matches awarded (+3, +7, etc.), quantity owned
Tap a contract → show player selector (squad builder grid) filtered to players who can receive this contract
Select player → confirm dialog: 'Apply {Silver Contract} to {Player Name}? (+7 matches)'
After apply: show success animation, update inventory, update player card contract badge
'Contract Packs' tab: list of unopened contract packs with pack name, count, source badge
Tap contract pack → opening animation (similar to card pack opening but simpler)
Pack opening shows each contract type awarded with tier badge and quantity
Player card detail screen (card_detail_screen.dart) shows: contracts_remaining / contracts_max with progress bar
Squad builder shows contract chip on each player card with color coding (green/yellow/red)
New route /contracts added to GoRouter shell routes with bottom nav entry
Loading states: shimmer while inventory loads, empty state illustration when no contracts
stories.technicalNotes: Reuse pack opening UI patterns from PackOpeningScreen. The player selector for contract application reuses the squad builder grid widget (extract as reusable component). Contract progress bar on card detail: green 50-100%, yellow 20-49%, red 0-19%. The apply contract flow is: select contract → select target → confirm → call RPC → refresh providers. Add route to bottom nav or under 'More' section.
edgeCases: Player already at max contracts — contract type should be grayed out / not selectable
Player card no longer owned — exclude from selector
Contract inventory empty — show 'Earn contracts by winning matches!' CTA button
Contract packs empty — show 'Buy contract packs in the store!' CTA
Applying a contract that would overflow contracts_max — just cap at max (don't lose matches)

stories.title: Contract packs as match rewards across all modes
stories.userStory: As a player, I want to earn contract packs by winning matches in any mode so that I'm incentivized to play more.
acceptanceCriteria: Match win in any mode rewards a contract pack (alongside existing coins/XP)
Contract pack tier varies by match difficulty/mode: Village (Bronze), Domestic (Bronze/Silver), International (Silver/Gold), Tournament (Gold/Elite), Multiplayer (Elite/Legend)
Losses in easier modes give nothing; losses in harder modes have a small chance of a Bronze pack
Contract packs are awarded via the `award_match_rewards` RPC (modified to also insert into user_contract_packs)
Daily reward system can optionally include contract packs on streak milestones
Level-up rewards can optionally include contract packs (e.g., level 10 → Silver Contract Pack)
Post-match result screen shows awarded contract pack with animation alongside coins/XP (if earned)
Awarded contract packs have source='reward' (or source='level_up_X' for level-ups)
stories.technicalNotes: Modify `award_match_rewards` RPC to accept a contract_pack_name parameter (nullable). Call from match_completion_handler. Use AppConstants.contractPackForDifficulty(difficulty, won) to determine the pack. This follows existing reward RPC pattern exactly. Multiplayer wins could grant cross-tier packs (e.g., 'Dynamic Contract Pack' with wider probability range).
edgeCases: Draw result — award a Bronze Contract Pack (participation reward)
User already has the same pack type — still create a new row (packs are individual, not stackable)
Very high-level users — Legend Contract Packs should feel rare even at max difficulty
Multiplayer ranked vs unranked — ranked wins give higher tier packs

stories.title: Contract packs in the store + market trading for contracts
stories.userStory: As a player, I want to buy contract packs from the store and trade extra contracts on the market so that I have multiple ways to acquire them.
acceptanceCriteria: Store screen shows contract packs section with coin and premium token prices
Contract pack types: Bronze Pack (100 coins), Silver Pack (500 coins), Gold Pack (2500 coins), Elite Pack (10000 coins / 50 tokens), Legend Pack (25000 coins / 150 tokens)
Bought packs go to user_contract_packs with source='purchase'
Gold+ tier contracts are tradeable on the market
Market listing for contracts: select quantity (1-10), set price per unit, 5% tax applies
Market buy: purchase individual contracts (not packs), added to user_contracts
Market listing type: 'contract' in transactions table reference
stories.technicalNotes: Store buying contracts follows the pack_provider.dart pattern almost exactly — check balance, deduct, insert into user_contract_packs. Market trading for contracts is simpler than card trading since contracts are stackable — just update quantity on the user_contracts row.
edgeCases: Market listing partial quantity — user lists 5, buyer buys 3 — should decrement listing quantity and add 3 to buyer
Price floor per tier: Bronze 10, Silver 50, Gold 200, Elite 1000, Legend 5000 coins
Applied contracts lose tradeable status (consumable once applied)
